examples.title: Sequential Access Algorithm
examples.description: Demonstrates how sequential file access works by reading records one by one from the beginning
examples.code:
PROCEDURE SequentialRead(fileHandle)
  BEGIN
    SET position = 0
    WHILE NOT EndOfFile(fileHandle) DO
      record = ReadRecord(fileHandle, position)
      PROCESS(record)
      position = position + 1
    END WHILE
    CloseFile(fileHandle)
  END

// Time Complexity: O(n) where n = number of records
// Space Complexity: O(1) - only stores current record
examples.explanation: This algorithm demonstrates sequential access where records are read in consecutive order. Each iteration reads the next record in sequence. The position counter increments to move to the next record. This is most efficient for batch processing where all records must be processed.

examples.title: Direct Access Algorithm (Relative Access)
examples.description: Shows how to calculate and access any record directly using the relative record number
examples.explanation: Direct access calculates the exact byte position using the formula: offset = recordNumber × recordSize. This allows immediate access to any record without reading preceding records. The seek operation positions the read head, then the record is read directly.

examples.title: Indexed Access Algorithm
examples.description: Demonstrates two-step access process: first read index to find block pointer, then read actual data block
examples.explanation: Indexed access provides efficient random access through a two-level lookup. The index block acts as a table of contents for data blocks. This approach supports both fast random access and sequential scanning by traversing the index. Modern file systems use variations of this approach.

examples.title: Contiguous File Allocation
examples.description: Shows allocation and access for contiguously stored files
examples.code:
PROCEDURE AllocateContiguous(fileName, fileSize)
  BEGIN
    // Find contiguous free blocks large enough
    startBlock = FindFreeContiguousBlocks(fileSize)
    
    IF startBlock == -1 THEN
      RETURN ERROR // Not enough contiguous space
    END IF
    
    // Allocate blocks and update free space bitmap
    FOR i = 0 TO fileSize-1 DO
      MarkBlockAllocated(startBlock + i)
      UpdateFAT(startBlock + i, i+1) // Optional: for recovery
    END FOR
    
    // Create file control block
    fcb = CreateFCB(fileName, startBlock, fileSize)
    RETURN fcb
  END

PROCEDURE ReadContiguous(fcb, position)
  BEGIN
    // Calculate physical block number
    blockNumber = fcb.startBlock + position
    
    // Single seek to read block
    Seek(blockNumber)
    record = ReadBlock()
    RETURN record
  END

// Sequential Access: O(1) per consecutive record
// Direct Access: O(1) per random access
// No pointer overhead, minimal disk seeks
examples.explanation: Contiguous allocation provides the best performance for both sequential and direct access. The starting block address plus offset gives any record's location immediately. However, it suffers from external fragmentation and difficulty in file expansion, requiring file relocation in some cases.
